notes:
RE-FETCH RESULTS (adversarial):
- ThePriceDex sm9 (PRIMARY): fetched OK. Confirms EXACTLY the cited per-pack odds: Secret Rare 1/120, Rainbow Rare 1/60, Ultra Rare 1/24, Rare Holo GX 1/10, Rare Holo 1/6, Rare 1/1.5. Also lists Rare Prism Star 1/18 (not separately in draft) — Prism Star = non-holo rare, lumped into "rare" by TCGdex coarse buckets, so correctly absorbed in the rare remainder. Cards/pack listed as 11 (counts the code/energy card); game cards = 10.
- Pokemon.com ETB gallery: fetched OK. Confirms ETB = 8 packs. etbPacks=8 verified.
- Pokemon Center booster display box page: confirms standard 36-pack Booster Display Box exists for Team Up; pack = "10 cards and 1 basic Energy". displayPacks=36 verified. Team Up is a STANDARD set (TAG TEAM era), so the 36-pack box is correct (not a special/no-box set).

PACK/SLOTS: 10 game cards = 4 common + 3 uncommon + 1 reverse-holo filler (common/uncommon 50/50 per baseline) + 1 rare-or-better; 10th basic Energy not modeled. packSize=10.

MAPPING ONTO COARSE BUCKETS (sum of fine odds, normalized to the always-resolving rare-or-better slot):
  ultra-rare = P(GX) 0.10000 + P(Ultra Rare full-art) 0.04167 = 0.14167
  secret-rare = P(Rainbow) 0.01667 + P(Gold/Secret) 0.00833 = 0.02500
  rare = 1 - 0.14167 - 0.02500 = 0.83333 (absorbs rare holo, non-holo rare, and prism star)
All four slot distributions sum to exactly 1.0 (verified: 0.83333+0.14167+0.025 = 1.00000).

HIT ECONOMY SANITY: ultra-rare ~14.2% (~1 GX-or-better / 7 packs), secret-rare ~2.5% (~1 / 40). Reasonable for a TAG-TEAM-heavy SM set; not over-claimed.

CONFIDENCE = medium: per-set aggregator figures (ThePriceDex), not a large published opening study, but per-set rather than era-average.
